     The Numbers Business Report

Judy (2019)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $24,319,961Details
International Box Office $21,175,039
Worldwide Box Office $45,495,000
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $2,131,622
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,945,685
Total $4,077,307
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Winter 1968 and showbiz legend Judy Garland arrives in Swinging London to perform a 6-week sold-out run at The Talk of the Town. It is 30 years since she shot to global stardom in The Wizard of Oz, but if her voice has weakened, its dramatic intensity has only grown. As she prepares for the show, battles with management, charms musicians and reminisces with friends and adoring fans, her wit and warmth shine through. Even her dreams of love seem undimmed as she embarks on a whirlwind romance with Mickey Deans, her soon-to-be fifth husband. Featuring some of her best-known songs, the film celebrates the voice, the capacity for love, and the sheer pizzazz of “the world’s greatest entertainer.”

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$2,916,548 (12.0% of total gross)
Legs:5.29 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:53.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Theater counts:461 opening theaters/1,627 max. theaters, 4.7 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $31,753,155

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 27th, 2019 (Limited) by Roadside Attractions

October 18th, 2019 (Wide) (Australia)
Video Release: December 10th, 2019 by Lionsgate Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for substance abuse, thematic content, some strong language, and smoking.
(Rating bulletin 2576 (Cert #51854), 5/1/2019)
Running Time: 118 minutes

Time period setting: 1950s, 1960s
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: LD Entertainment, Roadside Attractions, Pathe, BBC Films, Ingenious Media, Calamity Films
Production Countries: United Kingdom
Languages: English

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.


  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

Winter 1968 and showbiz legend Judy Garland arrives in Swinging London to perform a 6-week sold-out run at The Talk of the Town. It is 30 years since she shot to global stardom in The Wizard of Oz, but if her voice has weakened, its dramatic intensity has only grown. As she prepares for the show, battles with management, charms musicians and reminisces with friends and adoring fans, her wit and warmth shine through. Even her dreams of love seem undimmed as she embarks on a whirlwind romance with Mickey Deans, her soon-to-be fifth husband. Featuring some of her best-known songs, the film celebrates the voice, the capacity for love, and the sheer pizzazz of “the world’s greatest entertainer.”

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$2,916,548 (12.0% of total gross)
Legs:5.29 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:53.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Theater counts:461 opening theaters/1,627 max. theaters, 4.7 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $31,753,155

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 27th, 2019 (Limited) by Roadside Attractions
International Releases: October 4th, 2019 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
October 11th, 2019 (Wide) (Portugal)
October 17th, 2019 (Wide) (New Zealand)
October 17th, 2019 (Wide), released as Джуди (Russia (CIS))
October 18th, 2019 (Wide) (Australia)
Video Release: December 10th, 2019 by Lionsgate Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for substance abuse, thematic content, some strong language, and smoking.
(Rating bulletin 2576 (Cert #51854), 5/1/2019)
Running Time: 118 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Prologue
Plot point: Addiction, Biography, Comeback, Dysfunctional Family, Performing Arts, Single Parent
Social setting: Movie Business
Subgenre: Biographical Drama
Time period setting: 1950s, 1960s
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: LD Entertainment, Roadside Attractions, Pathe, BBC Films, Ingenious Media, Calamity Films
Production Countries: United Kingdom
Languages: English
